summary: Listener (registered Democrat, Rockville, Council D7, MD-19, MD-8) is in choose-mode for the June 23 primary. Today's coverage was a four-episode series on Charlotte Crutchfield (House D19 incumbent, 2026 slate with Sen. Kramer + Del. Stewart). The Crutchfield arc is the first state-legislative deep-dive of the project; she has a real eight-year voting record and a real signature bill (HB 147 spousal-defense repeal, 2021 dilution + 2022 enactment), so the historical-record ask the listener has been making for three weeks could be answered substantively. The arc explicitly named the listener's three biggest standing questions (fiscal restriction, Wootton, sanctuary) and gave honest bottom-lines on each: Crutchfield is NOT a fiscal-discipline candidate; she has not made a public statement on Wootton because the closure is a Board of Ed matter; her caucus alignment makes her a likely supporter of MD's existing sanctuary framework, not an opponent.
themes: fiscal discipline vs. spending growth | The listener's operating value across all races. Today's Crutchfield arc gave the cleanest answer yet on a sitting D19 delegate: she is NOT a fiscal-discipline vote on the Blueprint, family-leave, or Medicaid expansions. The fiscal-discipline alternative on the District 19 House ballot, if any, is in the open Cullison seat field.

candidate full-history dossiers from before recent session | Crutchfield dossier walked Howard '86 -> BC Law '89 -> MD Bar 1991 -> Asst SA MoCo 1991-92 -> Gulfstream + GD 1993-2006 -> MoCo Dem Central Committee 2010-14 -> MSPB 2014-17 -> House from 1/9/2019. This is the format the listener asked for on 4/26.

concrete side-by-side policy comparison across the listener's actual races | Today's ep4 walked the District 19 field by 5 axes (Blueprint/fiscal, criminal justice, housing/zoning, transit/Metro, Wootton/MCPS) and named which slate member owns each axis. Format extends the 5/5 Hidayat-vs-Luedtke split-table treatment.

MCPS structural decisions affecting them as a Rockville parent | Wootton + rating system are still Board-of-Ed-not-General-Assembly questions. Crutchfield arc named that distinction explicitly and pointed the listener at the structural state-legislative question (would a delegate support legislation to constrain MCPS school-closure decisions or alter the State Board appeal process), not the policy-of-the-week question.

sanctuary policy as a litmus test | Honest answer for MoCo Democrats on the District 19 House ballot remains: no slate-incumbent has explicitly opposed MD's existing sanctuary framework. The arc said this plainly on-air rather than burying it.
open_questions: Is Andrew Friedson the candidate who stands for fiscal restriction and spending discipline?
Why does MCPS want to close and move Wootton High School, and what happens next?
What is Kramer's full historical voting record, including old news beyond recent sessions? — owed dossier; he is now next in the project queue per the Crutchfield arc closing line.
What are the documented policy differences between each candidate on my primary ballot?
Which Democratic primary candidate actually opposes sanctuary policy on illegal immigration?
What is the new MCPS rating system actually doing — what changes for my kid?
What was Crutchfield's specific Chicago-area high school? (Open after today's arc — not on the public record reviewed.)
What was Crutchfield's spouse's name and the date of his death? (Open after today's arc — Archives entry says 'widow, two children' with no further detail.)

Which open-seat District 19 House candidates (Tichy, Dasgupta if filed, Jennison if filed) hold sanctuary-policy-opposition or fiscal-discipline positions? (Owed forum-question once platforms are published.)
Was Crutchfield's 2025-26 committee move from Judiciary to Government/Labor/Elections voluntary or leadership-driven? (Press coverage reviewed did not isolate.)
underserved_topics: Council At-Large field — listener votes for At-Large but no At-Large candidate has been profiled yet; remains tier-1 priority.
MoCo Board of Education race — Wootton and grading-system questions actually live here, but no BoE candidate has been profiled.
MoCo State's Attorney, Sheriff, Clerk, Register, Orphans' Court — listener has voting rights and zero coverage.
U.S. Representative MD-8 — Raskin race has five filed Democrats; no episode yet.
Sen. Ben Kramer dossier — listener flagged him by name on 4/27; he is on Crutchfield's slate; an 8+-year Senate voting record is owed and the project queue puts him next.
Del. Vaughn Stewart — third member of the D19 slate; not yet given a dedicated arc.
Open-seat D19 third-Delegate field once platforms publish (Tichy filed; Dasgupta and Jennison considering as of last reporting).

The ACA marketplace quiet-downgrade story — 4/28 ep1, 4/29 ep3

The Beltsville Agricultural Research Center closure as a District 19 candidate-record hook — 4/27 ep4 and ep4.rewrite
Listener's own frustration about promised candidate deep-dives not delivering — already narrated back across at least five episodes; do not repeat as a framing device
The Friedson cash-on-hand $2.1M cycle-to-date number with the Glass/Jawando comparison — 5/3 ep3; do not re-quote without a fresh report number
The labor-endorsement landscape paragraph (SEIU 500 + UFCW 27 to Jawando, MCEA to Glass, IAFF 1664 to Friedson) — 5/3 ep3; do not re-paste verbatim

The Penn Glee Club / 'penndawn96' biographical hook — used heavily in 5/4 ep1 (Luedtke origins); do not redeploy in non-Luedtke episodes
The Battaglia first-clerkship anecdote — used as a Luedtke biographical anchor 5/4 ep1; do not redeploy outside Luedtke series
The 'red-headed stepchildren up here' Hidayat quote — used three times across 5/5 series (ep1, ep3, ep4); do not redeploy more than once next week
The 'first Muslim officer of MCPD' framing of Hidayat's 1999 swearing-in — used in 5/5 ep1 and ep2; do not lead a future episode with this line
The FAA Designated Pilot Examiner / Gaithersburg EA07 detail about Hidayat — used in 5/5 ep2; do not repeat verbatim
The 2012 ADL Shield Award detail about Hidayat — used in 5/5 ep2; do not repeat verbatim
The Hidayat-vs-Luedtke split-table format — used in 5/5 ep3 and ep4; do not redeploy as a head-to-head template within the next 7 days

The HB 147 'Love Is No Defense to Sexual Crimes' walkthrough including the 125-4 / Senate-dilution / 125-6 + 46-0 sequence — used 5/6 ep3; do not repeat as a Crutchfield achievement narrative in non-Crutchfield episodes within the next 7 days

The 'one of three Black women elected for the first time to represent Montgomery County in the House of Delegates' framing of Crutchfield's 2019 swearing-in (with Wilkins and Queen) — used 5/6 ep1; do not lead a future episode with this line
The Howard '86 -> BC Law '89 -> MD Bar 1991 educational-pipeline anecdote about Crutchfield — used 5/6 ep1 and ep2; do not redeploy outside Crutchfield arc

The Gulfstream Aerospace + General Dynamics Advanced Information Systems corporate-counsel-and-employee-relations 1993-2006 sequence about Crutchfield — used 5/6 ep2; do not repeat verbatim outside Crutchfield arc

The 'no public Crutchfield statement on Wootton because the closure is a Board of Ed matter, not General Assembly' line — used 5/6 ep3 and ep4; reusable next week ONLY in the structural state-legislative-vs-county-ballot framing, not as a Crutchfield-specific takedown

The 5-axis policy-disagreement decomposition (Blueprint/fiscal, criminal justice, housing/zoning, transit/Metro, Wootton/MCPS) — used 5/6 ep4; do not redeploy as a slate-comparison template within the next 7 days
The 'if she/he wins what changes in your daily life — continuity vs. break' close — used 5/6 ep4 and earlier; vary close-line for next week's series
